text_original: Motse said : Those gentlemen in the world who want to practise magnanimity and righteousness cannot but examine the origin of righteousness.
text_normalized: Motse said : Those gentlemen in the world who want to practise magnanimity and righteousness cannot but examine the origin of righteousness.

text_original: Since we want to examine the origin of righteousness, then where does it originate ?
text_normalized: Since we want to examine the origin of righteousness, then where does it originate ?

text_original: Motse said : Righteousness does not originate with the stupid and humble but with the honourable and wise.
text_normalized: Motse said : Righteousness does not originate with the stupid and humble but with the honourable and wise.

text_original: How do we know it does not originate with the dull and humble but with the honourable and wise ?
text_normalized: How do we know it does not originate with the dull and humble but with the honourable and wise ?

text_original: For, righteousness is the standard.
text_normalized: For, righteousness is the standard.

text_original: How do we know righteousness is the standard ?
text_normalized: How do we know righteousness is the standard ?

text_original: For, with righteousness the world will be orderly and without it the world will be disorderly.
text_normalized: For, with righteousness the world will be orderly and without it the world will be disorderly.

text_original: Therefore righteousness is known to be the standard.
text_normalized: Therefore righteousness is known to be the standard.

text_original: As the dull and the humble cannot make the standard, and only the wise and honourable can, therefore I know righteousness does not come from the stupid and humble but from the honourable and wise.
text_normalized: As the dull and the humble cannot make the standard, and only the wise and honourable can, therefore I know righteousness does not come from the stupid and humble but from the honourable and wise.

text_original: Now who is honourable and who is wise?
text_normalized: Now who is honourable and who is wise?

text_original: Heaven is honourable, Heaven is wise.
text_normalized: Heaven is honourable, Heaven is wise.

text_original: So, then, righteousness must originate with Heaven.
text_normalized: So, then, righteousness must originate with Heaven.

text_original: People in the world would say ; That the emperor is more honourable than the feudal lords and that the feudal lords are more honourable than the ministers, we clearly see.
text_normalized: People in the world would say ; That the emperor is more honourable than the feudal lords and that the feudal lords are more honourable than the ministers, we clearly see.

text_original: But that Heaven is more honour-able and wise than the emperor, we do not see.” Motse said : I know Heaven is more honourable and wise than the emperor for a reason : When the emperor practises virtue Heaven rewards, when the emperor does evil Heaven punishes.
text_normalized: But that Heaven is more honour-able and wise than the emperor, we do not see.” Motse said : I know Heaven is more honourable and wise than the emperor for a reason : When the emperor practises virtue Heaven rewards, when the emperor does evil Heaven punishes.

text_original: Wken there are disease and calamities the emperor will purify and bathe himself and prepare clean cakes and wine to do sacrifice and libation to Heaven and the spirits.
text_normalized: Wken there are disease and calamities the emperor will purify and bathe himself and prepare clean cakes and wine to do sacrifice and libation to Heaven and the spirits.

text_original: Heaven then removes them.
text_normalized: Heaven then removes them.

text_original: But I have not yet heard of Heaven invoking the emperor for blessing.
text_normalized: But I have not yet heard of Heaven invoking the emperor for blessing.

text_original: So I know Heaven is more honourable and wise than the emperor.
text_normalized: So I know Heaven is more honourable and wise than the emperor.

text_original: And, this is not all.
text_normalized: And, this is not all.

text_original: We also learn of this from the book of the ancient kings which instructs us the vast and ineffable Tao of Heaven.
text_normalized: We also learn of this from the book of the ancient kings which instructs us the vast and ineffable Tao of Heaven.

text_original: It says : ‘‘ Brilliant and perspicacious Heaven on High, who enlightens and watches over the earth below !
text_normalized: It says : ‘‘ Brilliant and perspicacious Heaven on High, who enlightens and watches over the earth below !

text_original: This shows Heaven is more honourable and wise than the emperor.
text_normalized: This shows Heaven is more honourable and wise than the emperor.

text_original: But is there yet any one more honourable and wise than Heaven ?
text_normalized: But is there yet any one more honourable and wise than Heaven ?

text_original: Heaven is really the most honourable and wise.
text_normalized: Heaven is really the most honourable and wise.

text_original: Therefore, righteousness surely comes from Heaven.
text_normalized: Therefore, righteousness surely comes from Heaven.

text_original: And hence Motse said : If the gentlemen of the world really desire to follow the way and benefit the people, they must not disobey the will of Heaven, the origin of magnanimity and righteousness.
text_normalized: And hence Motse said : If the gentlemen of the world really desire to follow the way and benefit the people, they must not disobey the will of Heaven, the origin of magnanimity and righteousness.

text_original: Now that we must obey the will of Heaven, what does the will of Heaven desire and what does it abominate ?
text_normalized: Now that we must obey the will of Heaven, what does the will of Heaven desire and what does it abominate ?

text_original: Motse said : The will of Heaven abominates the large state which attacks small states, the large house which molests small houses, the strong who plunder the weak, the clever who deceive the stupid, and the honoured who disdain the humble — these are what the will of Heaven abominates.
text_normalized: Motse said : The will of Heaven abominates the large state which attacks small states, the large house which molests small houses, the strong who plunder the weak, the clever who deceive the stupid, and the honoured who disdain the humble — these are what the will of Heaven abominates.

text_original: On the other hand, it desires people having energy to work for each other, those knowing the way to teach each other, These are the two opening lines of the ode “ Hsiao Ming ” in Shih Ching.
text_normalized: On the other hand, it desires people having energy to work for each other, those knowing the way to teach each other, These are the two opening lines of the ode “ Hsiao Ming ” in Shih Ching.

text_original: Legge, vol. iv, p.
text_normalized: Legge, vol. iv, p.

text_original: 363, renders it thus : — and those possessing wealth to share with each other.
text_normalized: 363, renders it thus : — and those possessing wealth to share with each other.

text_original: And it desires the superior diligently to attend to government and the subordinates diligently to attend to their work.
text_normalized: And it desires the superior diligently to attend to government and the subordinates diligently to attend to their work.

text_original: When the superior attends to the government diligently, the country will be orderly.
text_normalized: When the superior attends to the government diligently, the country will be orderly.

text_original: When the subordinates attend to work diligently, wealth will be abundant.
text_normalized: When the subordinates attend to work diligently, wealth will be abundant.

text_original: When the country is orderly and wealth is abundant, within the state there will be wherewith to prepare clean cakes and wine to sacrifice to God and the spirits, and in relation with outside countries there will be wherewith to furnish rings, stones, pearls, and jades by which to befriend surrounding neighbours.
text_normalized: When the country is orderly and wealth is abundant, within the state there will be wherewith to prepare clean cakes and wine to sacrifice to God and the spirits, and in relation with outside countries there will be wherewith to furnish rings, stones, pearls, and jades by which to befriend surrounding neighbours.

text_original: With the grudges of the feudal lords inactive and fighting on the borders suspended, and the people within provided with food and rest, the emperor and the ministers and the superiors and subordinates will be gracious and loyal respec-tively, and father and son and elder and younger brothers will be affectionate and filial respectively.
text_normalized: With the grudges of the feudal lords inactive and fighting on the borders suspended, and the people within provided with food and rest, the emperor and the ministers and the superiors and subordinates will be gracious and loyal respec-tively, and father and son and elder and younger brothers will be affectionate and filial respectively.

text_original: Therefore when the principle of obeying the will of Heaven is understood and widely practised in the world, then justice and govern-ment will be orderly, the multitudes will be harmonious, the country will be wealthy, the supplies will be plenteous, and the people will be warmly clothed and sufficiently fed, peaceful and without worry.
text_normalized: Therefore when the principle of obeying the will of Heaven is understood and widely practised in the world, then justice and govern-ment will be orderly, the multitudes will be harmonious, the country will be wealthy, the supplies will be plenteous, and the people will be warmly clothed and sufficiently fed, peaceful and without worry.

text_original: Therefore Motse said: If the gentlemen of the world really desire to follow the way and benefit the people they must carefully investigate the principle that the will of Heaven is the origin of magnanimity and righteousness.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said: If the gentlemen of the world really desire to follow the way and benefit the people they must carefully investigate the principle that the will of Heaven is the origin of magnanimity and righteousness.

text_original: The rule of Heaven over the world is not unlike the rule of the feudal lord over the state.
text_normalized: The rule of Heaven over the world is not unlike the rule of the feudal lord over the state.

text_original: In ruling the state does mutual disadvantage ?
text_normalized: In ruling the state does mutual disadvantage ?

text_original: If leading a large state one attacks small states, if leading a large tonse one molests small houses — if by doing this one seeks reward and commendation (from the feudal lord) he cannot obtain it.
text_normalized: If leading a large state one attacks small states, if leading a large tonse one molests small houses — if by doing this one seeks reward and commendation (from the feudal lord) he cannot obtain it.

text_original: On the contrary, punishment will visit him.
text_normalized: On the contrary, punishment will visit him.

text_original: Now, the rule of Heaven over the world is not unlike this.
text_normalized: Now, the rule of Heaven over the world is not unlike this.

text_original: If leading a large state one attacks small states, if leading a large house one molests small houses — if by doing this one seeks reward and com-mendation (from Heaven) he cannot obtain it.
text_normalized: If leading a large state one attacks small states, if leading a large house one molests small houses — if by doing this one seeks reward and com-mendation (from Heaven) he cannot obtain it.

text_original: On the contrary, punishment will visit him.
text_normalized: On the contrary, punishment will visit him.

text_original: When (man) does not do what Heaven desires, but does what Heaven abominates, Heaven will also not do what man desires but do what he abominates.
text_normalized: When (man) does not do what Heaven desires, but does what Heaven abominates, Heaven will also not do what man desires but do what he abominates.

text_original: What man abominates are disease and calamities.
text_normalized: What man abominates are disease and calamities.

text_original: Therefore not to do what Heaven desires but do what it abominates is to lead the multitudes in the world to calamity.
text_normalized: Therefore not to do what Heaven desires but do what it abominates is to lead the multitudes in the world to calamity.

text_original: Therefore the ancient sage-kings studied what Heaven and the spirits would bless and avoided what they would curse in order to procure benefits for the world and avoid calamities.
text_normalized: Therefore the ancient sage-kings studied what Heaven and the spirits would bless and avoided what they would curse in order to procure benefits for the world and avoid calamities.

text_original: Thereupon, Heaven made heat and cold temperate, the four seasons harmonious, the Yin and Yang and rain and dew timely.
text_normalized: Thereupon, Heaven made heat and cold temperate, the four seasons harmonious, the Yin and Yang and rain and dew timely.

text_original: The five grains are enabled to ripen and the six animals to mature.
text_normalized: The five grains are enabled to ripen and the six animals to mature.

text_original: And disease, pestilence, and famine did not happen.
text_normalized: And disease, pestilence, and famine did not happen.

text_original: Therefore Motse said : If the gentlemen of the world really desire to follow the Tao and benefit the people they must be mindful of the principle that the will of Heaven is the origin of magnanimity and righteousness.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said : If the gentlemen of the world really desire to follow the Tao and benefit the people they must be mindful of the principle that the will of Heaven is the origin of magnanimity and righteousness.

text_original: In the world those who lack benevolence are unfortunate.
text_normalized: In the world those who lack benevolence are unfortunate.

text_original: If a son does not serve his father, a younger brother does not serve his elder brother, or a subordinate does not serve WILL OF HIAVBN (II) his superior then all the gentlemen of the world will call him xmfortnnate.
text_normalized: If a son does not serve his father, a younger brother does not serve his elder brother, or a subordinate does not serve WILL OF HIAVBN (II) his superior then all the gentlemen of the world will call him xmfortnnate.

text_original: Now Heayen loves the whole world universally.
text_normalized: Now Heayen loves the whole world universally.

text_original: Everything is prepared  for the good of man.
text_normalized: Everything is prepared  for the good of man.

text_original: Even the tip of a hair is the work of Heaven.
text_normalized: Even the tip of a hair is the work of Heaven.

text_original: Substantial may be said of the benefits that are enjoyed by man.
text_normalized: Substantial may be said of the benefits that are enjoyed by man.

text_original: Yet there is no service in return.
text_normalized: Yet there is no service in return.

text_original: And they do not even know this to be nmnagnanimons and unfortunate.
text_normalized: And they do not even know this to be nmnagnanimons and unfortunate.

text_original: This is why I say the gentlemen understand only trifles and not things of importance.
text_normalized: This is why I say the gentlemen understand only trifles and not things of importance.

text_original: Moreover I know Heaven loves men dearly not without reason.
text_normalized: Moreover I know Heaven loves men dearly not without reason.

text_original: Heaven ordered the sun, the moon, and the stars to enlighten and guide them.
text_normalized: Heaven ordered the sun, the moon, and the stars to enlighten and guide them.

text_original: Heaven ordained the four seasons, Spring, Autumn, Winter, and Summer, to regulate them.
text_normalized: Heaven ordained the four seasons, Spring, Autumn, Winter, and Summer, to regulate them.

text_original: Heaven sent down snow, frost, rain, and dew to grow the five grains and flax and silk  that so the people could use and enjoy them.
text_normalized: Heaven sent down snow, frost, rain, and dew to grow the five grains and flax and silk  that so the people could use and enjoy them.

text_original: Heaven established the hills and rivers, ravines and valleys, and arranged many things to minister to man’s good or bring him evil.
text_normalized: Heaven established the hills and rivers, ravines and valleys, and arranged many things to minister to man’s good or bring him evil.

text_original: He appointed the dukes and lords to reward the virtuous and punish the wicked, and to gather metal and wood, birds and beasts, and to engage in cultivating the five grains and flax and silk to provide for the people’s food and clothing.
text_normalized: He appointed the dukes and lords to reward the virtuous and punish the wicked, and to gather metal and wood, birds and beasts, and to engage in cultivating the five grains and flax and silk to provide for the people’s food and clothing.

text_original: This has been taking place from antiquity to the present.
text_normalized: This has been taking place from antiquity to the present.

text_original: Suppose there is a man who is deeply fond of his son and has used his energy to the limit to work for his benefit.
text_normalized: Suppose there is a man who is deeply fond of his son and has used his energy to the limit to work for his benefit.

text_original: But when the son grows up he returns no love to the father.
text_normalized: But when the son grows up he returns no love to the father.

text_original: The gentlemen of the world will all call him unmagnanimous and miserable.
text_normalized: The gentlemen of the world will all call him unmagnanimous and miserable.

text_normalized: Now Heaven is not ascertainable. loves the whole world universaUy.

text_original: Yet this does not exhaust my reasons whereby I know Heaven loves man dearly.
text_normalized: Yet this does not exhaust my reasons whereby I know Heaven loves man dearly.

text_normalized: From whom is the visitation?

text_normalized: If Heaven does not love the people dearly, why should Heaven send down a visitation upon the man who murders the innocent ?

text_original: Thus I know Heaven loves man dearly.
text_normalized: Thus I know Heaven loves man dearly.

text_original: They are the ancient sage-kings of the Three Dynasties, Yao, Shun, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu.
text_normalized: They are the ancient sage-kings of the Three Dynasties, Yao, Shun, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu.

text_original: What did Yao, Shun, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu do ?
text_normalized: What did Yao, Shun, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu do ?

text_original: They engaged themselves in universality and not partiality (in love).
text_normalized: They engaged themselves in universality and not partiality (in love).

text_original: Loving universally, they did not attack the small states with their large states, they did not molest the small houses with their large houses.
text_normalized: Loving universally, they did not attack the small states with their large states, they did not molest the small houses with their large houses.

text_original: The strong did not plunder the weak, the many did not oppress the few, the clever did not deceive the stupid, the honoured did not disdain the humble.
text_normalized: The strong did not plunder the weak, the many did not oppress the few, the clever did not deceive the stupid, the honoured did not disdain the humble.

text_original: Such a regime was agreeable to Heaven above, to the spirits in the middle sphere, and to the people below.
text_normalized: Such a regime was agreeable to Heaven above, to the spirits in the middle sphere, and to the people below.

text_original: Being helpful to these three, it was helpful to all.
text_normalized: Being helpful to these three, it was helpful to all.

text_original: And this was Heavenly virtue.
text_normalized: And this was Heavenly virtue.

text_original: The most excellent names in the world were gathered and attributed to them, and they were called magnanimous, righteous, beloved of man and beneficial to the people, obedient to the will of Heaven and rewarded of Heaven.
text_normalized: The most excellent names in the world were gathered and attributed to them, and they were called magnanimous, righteous, beloved of man and beneficial to the people, obedient to the will of Heaven and rewarded of Heaven.

text_original: Therefore the will of Heaven is like the compasses to the wheelwright and the square to the carpenter.

text_original: The wheel-wright tests the circularity of every object in the world with his compasses, saying : '' That which satisfies my compasses is circular.

text_original: The carpenter also tests the squareness of every object in the world witli Hs square, saying : '' Tliat wMch satisfies my square is square ; that which does not is not square.'' There-fore whether any object is square or not is all Imown.
text_normalized: The carpenter also tests the squareness of every object in the world witli Hs square, saying : '' Tliat wMch satisfies my square is square ; that which does not is not square.'' There-fore whether any object is square or not is all Imown.

text_original: Because the standard of squareness is established.
text_normalized: Because the standard of squareness is established.

text_original: Similarly, with the will of Heaven Motse will measure the jurisdiction and government of the lords in the empire on the one hand, and the doctrines and teachings of the multitudes in the empire on the other.
text_normalized: Similarly, with the will of Heaven Motse will measure the jurisdiction and government of the lords in the empire on the one hand, and the doctrines and teachings of the multitudes in the empire on the other.

text_original: If some conduct is observed to be in accordance with the will of Heaven, it is called good conduct ; if it is in opposition to the will of Heaven it is called bad conduct.
text_normalized: If some conduct is observed to be in accordance with the will of Heaven, it is called good conduct ; if it is in opposition to the will of Heaven it is called bad conduct.

text_original: If a teaching is observed to be in accordance with the will of Heaven it is called good teaching ; if it is in opposition to the will of Heaven it is called bad teaching.
text_normalized: If a teaching is observed to be in accordance with the will of Heaven it is called good teaching ; if it is in opposition to the will of Heaven it is called bad teaching.

text_original: And if a government is observed to be in accordance with the will of Heaven it is called good government ; if it is in opposition to the will of Heaven it is called bad government.
text_normalized: And if a government is observed to be in accordance with the will of Heaven it is called good government ; if it is in opposition to the will of Heaven it is called bad government.

text_original: With this as the model and with this as the standard, whether the lords and the ministers are magnanimous or not can be measured as (easily as) to distinguish black and white.
text_normalized: With this as the model and with this as the standard, whether the lords and the ministers are magnanimous or not can be measured as (easily as) to distinguish black and white.

text_original: Therefore Motse said : If the rulers and the gentlemen of the world really desire to follow the way and benefit the people they have only to obey the will of Heaven, the origin of magnanimity and righteousness.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said : If the rulers and the gentlemen of the world really desire to follow the way and benefit the people they have only to obey the will of Heaven, the origin of magnanimity and righteousness.

text_original: Obedience to the will of Heaven is the standard of righteousness.
text_normalized: Obedience to the will of Heaven is the standard of righteousness.
